mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 4.0% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rising inflation signals stagflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as both growth and inflation risks weigh on valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in co-declining INDPRO/CPI scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals weak demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as consumer spending expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or housing starts recover without equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in real GDP above 3% signals strong economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in high-GDP growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as borrowing costs and recession risks increase.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or rate hikes occur without equity market declines.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll growth and declining unemployment signals a tight labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through elevated corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in co-movement scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 100 combined with rising inflation signals supply-side shocks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as stagflation risks materialize.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in co-declining INDPRO/inflation scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
